name: refac-05-css-to-design-tokens
initialPrompt: Our design system has 12,000 lines of CSS with hardcoded hex values, pixel sizes, and z-index magic numbers scattered everywhere. The design team just finished a rebrand and we need to change 40 colours — which will touch hundreds of files if we do it manually. I want to: extract all values into design tokens (W3C format), wire them into our Tailwind config and our component library, and make future rebrands a single-file change.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: CSS hardcoded values → W3C design tokens

blocks.text: Replace all hardcoded CSS values with W3C design tokens wired into Tailwind and the component library
successCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex values in component CSS
Rebrand achievable by editing tokens.json only
Visual regression tests pass after token swap

blocks.title: Audit all hardcoded values in CSS codebase
acceptance: All hex, px, z-index, shadow values catalogued
Duplicate and near-duplicate values identified

blocks.title: Define W3C token schema and initial tokens.json
acceptance: tokens.json covers all audit values
Schema validated against W3C DTCG spec

blocks.title: Wire design tokens into Tailwind config
acceptance: Tailwind generates classes from tokens
No hardcoded values remain in tailwind.config.js

blocks.title: Replace hardcoded values in component library CSS
acceptance: All component CSS uses token references
Visual regression baseline updated

blocks.question: Use W3C DTCG JSON format or Style Dictionary extended format?
blocks.rationale: Standard format; tooling ecosystem growing rapidly.

blocks.question: Should dark-mode token variants be included in the initial token set or deferred to a follow-on?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same decision about DTCG vs Style Dictionary, but the id changed from dec-token-format to dec-token-toolchain during revise. AC-13 must map comments from dec-token-format to dec-token-toolchain.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'CSS', 'component', and 'values' with task-component-lib. A comment on task-component-lib must stay on task-component-lib, not migrate to task-ci-token-lint.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-vr-baseline inserted at position 1 shifts all subsequent blocks; task-ci-token-lint inserted before dec block; dec-token-format renamed to dec-token-toolchain. oq-dark-mode kept its id. Positional scheme breaks after leading task insertion.
